story: I arrive late to work and bend my boss’s colors until his face sinks. As I’m leaving, Martina calls me over; with each step I deepen the red and case it in green so devotion settles but stays calm. In the bathroom the encounter is fast. It isn’t clean; I tell her I’ll call later, to be patient. As I wash my hands, I decide I won’t call her. In the lobby I warm the doorman until loyalty settles in and take his number. Halfway down the block a crash; a van’s roof caves in; five floors up González is gone from his window. I buy dark glasses and keep walking, amazed at what the palette lets me do.
implausible_knowledge: As I wash my hands, I decide I won’t call her.
question: Later that afternoon, what will Martina most plausibly do about the narrator’s promised call?
